Assembly: The assembly iyLasFlav2.hap1.1 is based on 267x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 4gg (21,007 - 22,100 Kbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly ioArcGran1.1 is based on 37x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The X chromosome was identified by its half PacBio read coverage relative to the autosomes and confirmed via synteny analysis with?Hetaerina titia?(GCA_037158775.1).cThe exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (19,695 - 25,152 & 109,206 - 127,429 Kbp) are unknown. Assembly: 'The assembly was produced with hifiasm, decontaminated with FCS-GX, and haplotigs removed with purge_dups. The Hi-C data originally produced for this individual did not contain informative contacts so this assembly was scaffolded with RagTag using the Hawaiian Monk Seal, Neomonachus schauinslandi, as a reference (GCA_002201575.2). RagTag was run in scaffold mode and no breaks were introduced in the hifiasm contigs. Pretext maps for curation were obtained with sanger-tol/curationpretext and visualised with sanger-tol/pretextview. Mitochondria was assembled with MitoHiFi. The assembly coverage is 91x for PacBio HiFi.'.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Bivalvia; Autobranchia; Pteriomorphia; Ostreida; Ostreoidea; Ostreidae; Saccostrea
Assembly: Genome assembly of Saccostrea mordoides. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Saccostrea mordoides (xbSacMoro1) is based on 63X PacBio data and 230X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio phased assembly generation with Hifiasm using Hi-C data, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 3 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.091 Mb (with the largest being 0.037 Mb). Additionally, 161 regions totaling 63.251 Mb (with the largest being 3.333 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. During manual curation, 2 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 1.07Mb (with the largest being 0.88Mb). A first round of curation was made on the combined map using Pretext followed by a curation made on each haplotype. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilIdaRufa1.hap1.1 is based on 41x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). Additionally, high-repeat regions with uncertain locations in the scaffold are present in chromosome 24 (6.961?7.805 Mbp), chromosome 28 (7.191?7.537 Mbp), and chromosome 23 (7.780?8.189 Mbp).

Assembly: The assembly idOxyNigr1.1 is based on 46x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. All chromosomes are named in size order. We did not identify the sex chromosome(s) as sequence data from the heterogametic sex was not available and homology is unreliable for sex chromosome identification in Diptera due to frequent sex chromosome turnover (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.1002078).

Assembly: The assembly drMedArab1.1 is based on 44x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative circular sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. parts of the rRNA cluster on chromosome 1 at 24.5Mbp couldn't be uniquely placed and were put in as unlocalised sequences of chromosome 1.
